outputs.test_harness_manifest: Synthetic planning manifest identifies two non-executed harness asset needs: one physical fixture for page-module verification alignment and one simulation harness for composed harness candidate readiness review. Manifest status is planning-only and does not assert availability, execution, validation, or TRR approval.
outputs.test_interface_list: Planned interfaces: physical fixture interface for module-to-fixture mechanical/electrical alignment; simulation harness interface for candidate input/output stimulus and observation points. Interface definitions remain placeholder-level because the synthetic contract provides no page-module pinout, protocol, timing, or acceptance details.
outputs.simulation_fixture_needs: One simulation harness is needed to model composed harness candidate behavior under synthetic stimulus. Required planning details include stimulus shape, observable outputs, pass/fail criteria owner, and traceability to verification readiness references. No simulation run is claimed.
outputs.instrumentation_resource_list: Instrumentation planning should reserve non-specific measurement and observation resources suitable for the physical fixture and simulation harness. Exact instruments, licenses, probes, data capture settings, and calibration records are owner-supplied follow-up inputs, not inferred from the synthetic contract.
outputs.trr_readiness_checklist: TRR readiness planning checklist: confirm harness asset ownership; confirm physical fixture requirement; confirm simulation harness requirement; resolve the planning blocker; obtain owner follow-up input; map planned interfaces to verification readiness refs; document boundary limits; explicitly record that no tests, simulations, or TRR approval occurred.
outputs.planning_blockers: One planning blocker is present: acceptance criteria and interface details are insufficient to complete concrete harness asset specifications beyond high-level physical and simulation needs.
outputs.owner_followup_needed: One owner follow-up item is needed: provide approved interface details, acceptance criteria, and resource constraints for the physical fixture and simulation harness so planning can be refined without crossing into execution or approval.
outputs.boundary_review_note: Public-safe synthetic output only. This plan preserves planning-only scope, readiness/blocker semantics, and boundary limits. It does not claim tool use, file edits, runtime paths, hidden evidence, upstream mutation, test execution, simulation execution, or TRR approval.

provenance: Public-safe synthetic workflow contract for test_harness_asset_planning_v0.
Synthetic scenario facts specifying one physical fixture need, one simulation harness need, one planning blocker, and one owner follow-up item.
gaps: No concrete page-module interface, protocol, timing, pinout, environmental, or acceptance criteria were provided.
No approved resource inventory, fixture design constraints, instrumentation constraints, or TRR authority decision criteria were provided.
downstream_handoff: Owner should supply approved interface and acceptance details before converting this planning manifest into asset specifications.
A later readiness review may use this plan as input, but must separately verify blockers, evidence, execution results, and approval authority.
boundary_review_note: Output is limited to public-safe synthetic planning semantics from the supplied contract.
No execution, mutation, hidden evidence, runtime path, tool-use, file-edit, canon promotion, or TRR approval claim is made.
no_claims: No tests or simulations were run or implied.
No upstream artifacts were edited, approved, promoted, or used as hidden evidence.
